title: Passports to Plett Rage now for sale
description: PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The iconic annual...
bodyText:
PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS - The iconic annual youth festival, Plett Rage, is back for another edition in its 25th year - set to be bigger, better and bolder, say organisers.

Celebrating its rich legacy and promising an unforgettable experience, Plett Rage 2024 is already being planned for the thousands of young revellers from across South Africa and beyond who attend.


> From 30 November to 6 December, Plettenberg Bay will transform into the ultimate party destination, welcoming thousands of youngsters.


Renowned for its vibrant atmosphere, stunning coastal backdrop, and unparalleled entertainment line-up, Plett Rage has solidified its status as the premier event on every young person's calendar, say Plett Tourism.

"Plett Rage 2024 invites you to a week filled with thrilling adventures, endless fun, and unforgettable moments. Our top priority remains the safety and enjoyment of every festival attendee, meticulously planned by our dedicated team. As matriculants and alumni gather, expect an epic celebration within a secure and controlled environment," shares Ronen Klugman, Plett Rage Festival owner.

"Anticipate an all-new Coco Valley venue, with enhanced production and immersive decor across our venues, accompanied by an extraordinary line-up that promises to exceed expectations."


> A mix of entertainment


Attendees can look forward to an eclectic mix of entertainment, including live performances by top local and international artists, epic beach parties, adrenaline-fuelled water sports, and exclusive after-parties that will keep the energy levels soaring into the early hours.

Artists already lined up are Jeremy Loops, Matthew Mole, Francois Van Coke, Jack Parow, YoungstaCPT, Kyle and Austin Cassim, with more to be announced.

In addition to the thrilling entertainment line-up, Plett Rage 2024 is committed to promoting responsible partying and safety among attendees. The organisers have implemented comprehensive security measures and partnered with local authorities to ensure a safe and secure environment for all festivalgoers.

"Plett Rage is not just a festival; it's a community where young people can come together to celebrate life, friendship, and the spirit of adventure," adds Klugman, "As always, we encourage our attendees to party responsibly, respect the local community, and make memories that will last a lifetime."


> Passports available to purchase


Passports for Plett Rage 2024 are now available for purchase, with various general and VIP packages and options to suit every budget and preference. Plett Rage passport info and options are available at plettrage.co.za.

CEO of Plett Tourism Patty Butterworth says Plett Rage is the 'grandaddy' of all South African rages and it is well known as the celebration for school-leavers.

"The event provides a positive economic boost for everyone. Plett Rage has helped put Plett as a destination on the map. The Plett Rage brand is big business, and has become very attractive to sponsors, and the bigger the festival, the greater the return on investment to the town for years to come. Corporate sponsors see the value of event offerings with future opportunities, the students enjoy a wonderful experience in Plett which in turn creates a desire to return in the future. The event offers opportunities for locals to work - building venue sets, assisting with security, providing customer relations, taxi services, food offerings and so much more.

"Most locals are very excited about the opening of the Plett summer season, which starts with the Plett Rage event. The town begins to fill up, local businesses are benefitting with the new invigoration of guests and the students offer a refreshing and optimistic vibe in town, something we need in these post-pandemic times.


> "Locals are well equipped to handle the students, Plettenberg Bay has been hosting students for 40+ years during this time of year – even before the formalisation of the Plett Rage brand.


"The Rage is about uniting so many like-minded, beautiful young adults under one incredible setting as a send-off for them, a special event for the future leaders and citizens of our country. The Plett Tourism Association encourages Plettonians to focus on the #oneplett philosophy and engage directly to understand and acknowledge facts before misrepresenting issues pertaining to our wonderful town and community."

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
